hypothesis: Enhancing selectivity for SSTR2 by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better engage SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3 residues, and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity ligands for SSTR2, but structural instability and off-target interactions are limiting selectivity. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions and tighter structural constraints are needed to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding interface or receptor-ligand complex. Reducing the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and improve structural quality.

To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions will allow us to fine-tune selectivity while preserving the core pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 + Cys3/Cys14).
